options: Sovereign power&nbsp;ultimately&nbsp;resides&nbsp;in the people, and they&nbsp;remain&nbsp;unlimited in their powers to command. /

Sovereign power&nbsp;ultimately resides&nbsp;in the government, and it is&nbsp;limited by constitutional law. /

questions.statement: Which of the following is not a tool of legislative control over administration?
Name the editor of the book, &quot;Towards a New Public Administration-Minnowbrook&quot;:
Who has authored the book titled &lsquo;Flying Blind: India&rsquo;s Quest for Global Leadership&rsquo;?

questions.explanation:
The correct answer is - 1, 2 and 4 only

Key Points

  • India’s “Neighbourhood First” Policy
    • This policy emphasizes giving priority to relations with South Asian neighbours, including countries like Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and others, ensuring peaceful and cooperative regional ties.
    • It focuses on economic integration, connectivity, and development partnerships to enhance regional prosperity and stability.
    • Regional cooperation is a key component, pursued through platforms such as BIMSTEC (Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation) and SAARC (South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation).
  • Incorrect Statement
    • Statement 3, which claims that the policy was first articulated during the UPA-II government, is incorrect. The “Neighbourhood First” policy was formally articulated and emphasized under the Modi government starting in 2014.

Additional Information

  • Key Components of Neighbourhood First Policy
    • Economic Integration: India supports regional trade and investments to enhance economic ties with its neighbours.
    • Connectivity: Infrastructure projects such as highways, railways, and ports are being developed to improve physical connectivity.
    • Development Partnerships: India extends financial and technical assistance to neighbouring countries for development projects, including energy, education, and health.
  • Regional Cooperation Forums
    • SAARC: Established in 1985, SAARC aims to promote regional cooperation among South Asian nations.
    • BIMSTEC: This forum connects South Asia with Southeast Asia, focusing on economic and technical collaboration in key areas like trade, energy, and technology.
  • Examples of Initiatives under Neighbourhood First Policy
    • BBIN Initiative: A sub-regional effort by Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, and Nepal to enhance connectivity and trade through road networks.
    • Assistance to Maldives: India has extended significant financial and technical aid to Maldives, particularly in areas like health, education, and infrastructure.
    • Energy Cooperation: India has provided electricity to Nepal and Bhutan through hydropower projects and energy grid connectivity.
